mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling or flat after a prior tightening phase, discount-rate pressure on equities eases and S&P 500 prices tend to re-rate upward over the next month. The signal is falsified if post-cut or flat-rate periods do not produce higher 20-day returns than unchanged-rates periods.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive more often than negative during falling FEDFUNDS months, or the rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent low marks labor-market deterioration, weakens household income expectations, and lowers equity risk appetite. The signal predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days after unemployment breaks upward.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points from its 6-month low
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE breakout events are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the sign of the average forward return is positive across triggered samples.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized increase in RSAFS and PAYEMS signals broad demand expansion through stronger retail sales and payroll growth, which supports earnings expectations and lifts equities. The effect should be positive over a one-quarter horizon and fail when either series weakens while the other rises.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous monthly improvements in RSAFS and PAYEMS do not outperform single-series signals, or if 60-day forward SPX returns are not positive in the top half of joint-growth observations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO after a prior trough indicates industrial output recovery and easing goods-side capacity stress, which improves cyclical earnings expectations and supports near-term equity gains. The signal is falsified if INDPRO accelerations are not followed by positive 5-day returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises month-over-month after at least two consecutive monthly declines
failure_condition: Reject if post-trough INDPRO rebounds fail to produce positive average 5-day SPX returns, or if the event hit-rate is not above 50% over a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL is rising but FEDFUNDS has not kept pace, real policy rates fall and equity valuations expand because expected tightening pressure is lower than inflation pressure. This predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next month and fails when inflation is accompanied by an equal or faster rise in FEDFUNDS.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth exceeds FEDFUNDS by at least 1 percentage point
regime_hint: disinflation lag or policy behind inflation
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-over-policy spread events do not produce positive median 20-day SPX returns, or if the signal loses sign consistency when FEDFUNDS rises as fast as CPIAUCSL.

mechanism_narrative: A falling PAYEMS level indicates payroll contraction and weaker aggregate income, which tends to reduce equity prices over the next quarter as earnings expectations are revised down. The hypothesis is falsified if payroll declines do not precede negative 60-day SPX returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines for two consecutive monthly releases
failure_condition: Reject if two-month payroll contraction periods are followed by non-negative 60-day SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the average forward return is not below zero.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts are a leading demand indicator: a sustained rise in HOUST reflects easier financing and improving household confidence, which translates into stronger cyclical equity performance over the next month. The signal fails if housing starts rises occur without subsequent positive returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises more than 5% from its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST breakout events do not deliver positive average 20-day SPX returns, or if the triggered win rate stays below 55% over any 3-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO is improving while UNRATE is stable or falling, supply-side activity is expanding without labor stress, a combination that supports earnings and keeps recession risk low. The mechanism predicts positive equity returns over 60 days and fails if industrial recovery coincides with rising unemployment.
failure_condition: Reject if joint instances of rising INDPRO and flat-to-lower UNRATE are not followed by positive 60-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the joint signal is weaker than either standalone proxy.
